summary: Frontend-only regression for Projects 3D cylinder fix. Verified all 6 projects can be reached and opened via Next/Prev controls, side-card click brings card to front, autoplay toggle works, fullscreen overlay works, drag rotation works, no runtime errors, no horizontal overflow on desktop (1440) or mobile (390). ALL acceptance criteria PASS.

test_results.controls_present: PASS (project-prev, project-next, project-autoplay, project-fullscreen-toggle all visible)
test_results.autoplay_toggle_label_change: PASS (Auto->Play on Next click, and toggles Auto<->Play on click)
test_results.reach_all_6_projects_via_next: PASS - stepped through and opened all 6 modals with matching titles/links: Mango, Sunflower, Real-Time Chat App, Cloud Vendor/Task Manager, Production AWS DevOps, DevOps Pipeline (SaaS). Includes the back-facing project that was previously unreachable.
test_results.side_card_click_brings_to_front: PASS - first click on a visible side card rotated it to front (opacity became max); second click opened correct modal
test_results.front_card_click_opens_modal: PASS - project-detail-modal shows correct title and project-detail-link has matching href
test_results.drag_rotation: PASS - drag changed the front card index
test_results.fullscreen_toggle: PASS - project-fullscreen-overlay opens; controls (prev/next/autoplay) visible inside; toggling back removes overlay

critical_code_review_comments: Projects.jsx: onClick handler on card uses `moved.current >= 6` guard to distinguish click vs drag; works but note that framer-motion drag/momentum isn't involved, using raw pointer. Fine.
Auto-rotate uses requestAnimationFrame with 0.16deg/frame (~9.6deg/s). Consider useReducedMotion for a11y (nice-to-have).
goToIndex/step both call setAutoPlay(false). Consider exposing a resume button in the copy hints — currently 'Play' label may confuse (typical convention: Auto=on, Paused=off). Minor UX.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Projects cylinder fix verified. If retesting, note the modal close animation needs wait_for_selector state='detached' before clicking Next again — otherwise the backdrop can absorb the click and Next won't advance.
